steps.title: Identify the extra detail
steps.content: The sentence names a specific path: the Harbor Park bicycle path. The clause about its length adds nonessential information, so it must be set off with commas on both sides before the sentence continues.

steps.title: Enclose the which clause
steps.content: Choice A has only the opening comma. Choice B has no commas, making the clause restrictive. Choice C has only the closing comma. The version with commas before and after the length clause correctly encloses the added detail. The answer is D.
